title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: The clearest signal today is a shift from static safety claims toward more operational evidence: labs are publishing new methods to simulate deployment, benchmark high-risk scientific capabilities, and harden agentic systems in practice. Health-domain evaluation also stands out, suggesting more domain-specific, expert-informed testing for model behavior where mistakes carry real-world consequences.

items.title: OpenAI proposes deployment simulation to predict model behavior before release
items.why_it_matters: Pre-deployment simulation could make safety evaluations more realistic than static benchmarks alone.

items.title: OpenAI introduces LifeSciBench for real-world life science capability evaluation
items.why_it_matters: A stronger bioscience benchmark helps assess dual-use and misuse-relevant model capabilities more credibly.

items.title: Google DeepMind outlines an AI Control Roadmap for securing agents
items.why_it_matters: Agent security controls and monitoring are becoming central to safe deployment of autonomous systems.

items.title: OpenAI details physician-informed evaluations for ChatGPT health responses
items.why_it_matters: Expert-informed health evaluations show how labs are tailoring safety testing to high-stakes domains.

items.title: Partnership on AI argues for stronger employee voice in company AI decisions
items.why_it_matters: Internal reporting and worker input can improve governance and surface deployment risks earlier.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
